title: `Fatal Python error` when using `_pickle` with many `frozenset` objects
author: devdanzin
body:
# Crash report

### What happened?

It's possible to make the interpreter abort when using `_pickle` with `frozenset` objects. Please let me know if the automated diagnosis is incorrect and whether you prefer me not to post it in new issues.

### Automated diagnosis:

Missing `fast_save_leave` bug in `save_frozenset` (_pickle.c:3674): `save_frozense`t calls `fast_save_enter` (line 3681) but never `calls fast_save_leave` on any return path. This causes:
1. `fast_nesting` to drift upward (never decremented for frozensets)
2. After `FAST_NESTING_LIMIT` (50) frozensets, `fast_memo` dict activates
3. Frozenset addresses get inserted into `fast_memo` but never removed
4. When an outer container (list/dict) calls `fast_save_leave`, it tries to remove itself from `fast_memo` via PyDict_DelItem — but it was never added. A frozenset was added at that nesting level instead.
5. `KeyError` propagates → `SystemError` → `Fatal Python error` → abort

Compare `with save_list` (line 3289) and `save_dict` (line 3588), which properly call fast_save_leave.


MRE:
```python
import _pickle
import io

buf = io.BytesIO()
p = _pickle.Pickler(buf, protocol=4)
p.fast = 1  # Enable fast mode (disables memo, enables cycle detection)

data = {"key": [frozenset([i]) for i in range(55)]}
p.dump(data)  # Fatal Python error: abort
```

Backtrace:
```
Fatal Python error: _Py_CheckFunctionResult: a function returned a result with an exception set
Python runtime state: initialized
KeyError: 136820327477664
when serializing dict item 'key'

The above exception was the direct cause of the following exception:

SystemError: <class 'KeyError'> returned a result with an exception set

Current thread 0x00007ffff7e4c3c0 [python] (most recent call first):
  File "/home/danzin/crashers/_pickle_frozenset_abort.py", line 33 in <module>

Program received signal SIGABRT, Aborted.

#0  __pthread_kill_implementation (threadid=<optimized out>, signo=6, no_tid=0) at ./nptl/pthread_kill.c:44
#1  __pthread_kill_internal (threadid=<optimized out>, signo=6) at ./nptl/pthread_kill.c:89
#2  __GI___pthread_kill (threadid=<optimized out>, signo=signo@entry=6) at ./nptl/pthread_kill.c:100
#3  0x00007ffff7c45e2e in __GI_raise (sig=sig@entry=6) at ../sysdeps/posix/raise.c:26
#4  0x00007ffff7c28888 in __GI_abort () at ./stdlib/abort.c:77
#5  0x0000555556007ad4 in fatal_error_exit (status=status@entry=-1) at Python/pylifecycle.c:3341
#6  0x0000555556007716 in fatal_error (fd=<optimized out>, header=<optimized out>, prefix=<optimized out>, msg=<optimized out>, status=<optimized out>) at Python/pylifecycle.c:3564
#7  0x0000555556003940 in _Py_FatalErrorFunc (func=0x555556325b20 <__func__._Py_CheckFunctionResult> "_Py_CheckFunctionResult",
    msg=0x555556325c80 <str> "a function returned a result with an exception set") at Python/pylifecycle.c:3587
#8  0x0000555555ab98d3 in _Py_CheckFunctionResult (tstate=0x5555568f7b18 <_PyRuntime+360664>, callable=0x5555566ad3a0 <_PyExc_KeyError>, result=<optimized out>, where=0x0)
    at Objects/call.c:65
#9  0x0000555555abcda5 in PyObject_CallOneArg (func=<optimized out>, arg=0x7c5ff7052990) at Objects/call.c:395
#10 0x0000555555f2eaf3 in _PyErr_SetKeyError (arg=arg@entry=0x7c5ff7052990) at Python/errors.c:256
#11 0x0000555555b99ef9 in _PyDict_DelItem_KnownHash_LockHeld (op=0x7c7ff70b4540, key=<optimized out>, hash=<optimized out>) at Objects/dictobject.c:2942
#12 0x00007bfff5abe01c in fast_save_leave (self=0x7cfff703f860, obj=obj@entry=0x7c7ff7088740) at ./Modules/_pickle.c:2212
#13 0x00007bfff5ab2b86 in save_dict (state=<optimized out>, self=<optimized out>, obj=0x7c7ff7088740) at ./Modules/_pickle.c:3588
#14 0x00007bfff5aaffc6 in save (st=<optimized out>, self=0x7cfff703f860, obj=<optimized out>, pers_save=<optimized out>) at ./Modules/_pickle.c:4568
#15 0x00007bfff5aadf00 in dump (state=<optimized out>, self=0x7cfff703f860, obj=<optimized out>) at ./Modules/_pickle.c:4768
#16 0x00007bfff5ad7c6c in _pickle_Pickler_dump_impl (self=0x7cfff703f860, cls=0x7d8ff7016ba0, obj=0x7c7ff7088740) at ./Modules/_pickle.c:4843
#17 _pickle_Pickler_dump (self=<optimized out>, cls=<optimized out>, args=0x7bfff5d47a28, nargs=<optimized out>, kwnames=<optimized out>) at ./Modules/clinic/_pickle.c.h:73
#18 0x0000555555bdf145 in cfunction_vectorcall_FASTCALL_KEYWORDS_METHOD (func=func@entry=0x7caff6fff900, args=args@entry=0x7bfff5d47a28, nargsf=nargsf@entry=9223372036854775809,
    kwnames=kwnames@entry=0x0) at Objects/methodobject.c:481
#19 0x0000555555ab9e00 in _PyObject_VectorcallTstate (tstate=0x5555568f7b18 <_PyRuntime+360664>, callable=0x7caff6fff900, args=0x7bfff5d47a28, nargsf=9223372036854775809, kwnames=0x0)
    at ./Include/internal/pycore_call.h:136
#20 0x0000555555e588dd in _Py_VectorCallInstrumentation_StackRefSteal (callable=..., arguments=<optimized out>, total_args=1, kwnames=..., call_instrumentation=<optimized out>,
    frame=<optimized out>, this_instr=<optimized out>, tstate=<optimized out>) at Python/ceval.c:770
#21 0x0000555555e94263 in _PyEval_EvalFrameDefault (tstate=<optimized out>, frame=<optimized out>, throwflag=<optimized out>) at Python/generated_cases.c.h:1838
#22 0x0000555555e57778 in _PyEval_EvalFrame (tstate=0x5555568f7b18 <_PyRuntime+360664>, frame=0x7e8ff6fe5220, throwflag=0) at ./Include/internal/pycore_ceval.h:118
#23 _PyEval_Vector (tstate=<optimized out>, func=<optimized out>, locals=<optimized out>, args=<optimized out>, argcount=<optimized out>, kwnames=0x0) at Python/ceval.c:2134
#24 0x0000555555e57195 in PyEval_EvalCode (co=<optimized out>, globals=<optimized out>, locals=0x7c7ff70884c0) at Python/ceval.c:681
#25 0x0000555556061fb0 in run_eval_code_obj (tstate=tstate@entry=0x5555568f7b18 <_PyRuntime+360664>, co=co@entry=0x7d4ff6ff0210, globals=globals@entry=0x7c7ff70884c0,
    locals=locals@entry=0x7c7ff70884c0) at Python/pythonrun.c:1368
```

### Claude explanation for the MRE and crash

The dict contains 55 frozensets. After the 50th frozenset, the cycle detection dict activates. When the outer dict's fast_save_leave runs, it tries to remove itself from fast_memo but finds a frozenset address there instead → KeyError → SystemError → Fatal Python error.

----

Found with [cpython-review-toolkit](https://github.com/devdanzin/cpython-review-toolkit) with Claude Opus 4.6, using the `/cpython-review-toolkit:explore Modules/_pickle.c all deep` command.



### CPython versions tested on:

CPython main branch

### Operating systems tested on:

Linux

### Output from running 'python -VV' on the command line:

Python 3.15.0a7+ (heads/main:99e2c5eccd2, Mar 17 2026, 08:26:50) [Clang 21.1.2 (2ubuntu6)]

comments.author: vstinner
comments.body: It's now fixed in the main branch by https://github.com/python/cpython/commit/5c0dcb3e0d817bd8c28e8efcdb97103cd9210989, backports to 3.13 and 3.14 will follow. Thanks for the bug report @devdanzin!
